channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: With strikes above and crackdowns on the ground, Iranians describe life under siege
item.description: A state-imposed internet blackout has obscured the reality of life in Iran as the war rages on. Those fleeing through neighboring countries share a rare glimpse of what life is like in Iran.
item.content:encoded: A state-imposed internet blackout has obscured the reality of life in Iran as the war rages on. Those fleeing through neighboring countries share a rare glimpse of what life is like in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Arezou Rezvani

item.title: Author Scott Anderson on Iran's power structure after death of top security official
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with journalist and author Scott Anderson about Iran's power structure after the killing of Ali Larijani, the head of Iran's Supreme National Security Council.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with journalist and author Scott Anderson about Iran's power structure after the killing of Ali Larijani, the head of Iran's Supreme National Security Council.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Rep. María Elvira Salazar, R-Fla., talks about Trump's comments on Cuba
item.description: Republican Congresswoman María Elvira Salazar of Florida is the daughter of Cuban exiles. NPR's Michel Martin asks her about President Trump's comments about the country.
item.content:encoded: Republican Congresswoman María Elvira Salazar of Florida is the daughter of Cuban exiles. NPR's Michel Martin asks her about President Trump's comments about the country.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Do you understand this billboard? If not, that's the whole point
item.description: San Francisco's streets are plastered with cryptic ads from AI startups. The strategy is intentional — but it's not without cost.
item.content:encoded:
San Francisco's streets are plastered with cryptic ads from AI startups. The strategy is intentional — but it's not without cost.

(Image credit: Chloe Veltman)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: The Federal Reserve is facing tough choices as the economy faces deep uncertainty
item.description: The Federal Reserve's job is expected to hold its benchmark interest rate steady as it faces inflationary pressure from the war with Iran — and a weakening labor market.
item.content:encoded:
The Federal Reserve's job is expected to hold its benchmark interest rate steady as it faces inflationary pressure from the war with Iran — and a weakening labor market.

(Image credit: Saul Loeb)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Iranians fleeing their country share rare glimpse of what life is like amid war, Trump floats idea of "taking" Cuba, the Federal Reserve is expected to hold its benchmark interest rate steady.
item.content:encoded: Iranians fleeing their country share rare glimpse of what life is like amid war, Trump floats idea of "taking" Cuba, the Federal Reserve is expected to hold its benchmark interest rate steady.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: ICE's detention expansion meets resistance in cities across the political spectrum
item.description: A massive expansion of immigration detention sites is under way, with multiple warehouse style facilities being built across the country. Many communities are pushing back against these new facilities.
item.content:encoded: A massive expansion of immigration detention sites is under way, with multiple warehouse style facilities being built across the country. Many communities are pushing back against these new facilities.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd

item.title: At least 400 dead in drug rehabilitation center strike in Kabul, Afghan officials say
item.description: Afghanistan says Pakistan is to blame for a strike on a drug rehabilitation center in Kabul that killed at least 400 people, the deadliest attack since fighting began weeks ago.
item.content:encoded: Afghanistan says Pakistan is to blame for a strike on a drug rehabilitation center in Kabul that killed at least 400 people, the deadliest attack since fighting began weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Betsy Joles

item.title: Once the airstrikes stop, Iran's nuclear threat leaves no easy endgame
item.description: Even after airstrikes end, Iran's nuclear threat looms and diplomacy may be too late.
item.content:encoded: Even after airstrikes end, Iran's nuclear threat looms and diplomacy may be too late.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: War with Iran strands about 20,000 seafarers in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Jacqueline Smith of the International Transport Workers' Federation about the roughly 20,000 seafarers stuck in the Strait of Hormuz in the midst of the Iran war.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Jacqueline Smith of the International Transport Workers' Federation about the roughly 20,000 seafarers stuck in the Strait of Hormuz in the midst of the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin
